title: Sign in — PlausiDen
description: Sign in with passkey, WebAuthn, magic link, or password.

sections.title: Welcome back.
sections.lede: Use a passkey, your security key, or sign in with email.

sections.title: Sign in to PlausiDen
sections.tagline: Pick a method. We default to passkey — it's phishing-resistant by construction.
sections.footer: By signing in you accept the Terms + Privacy policy. We never see your prompts or content.

sections.title: Enter your code
sections.instructions: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.

sections.prompt: Select all photos that contain a bird.
sections.attribution_hint: Your response helps train PlausiDen-LFI. Curated for the open corpus.

sections.text: Forge-auth ships nine typed AuthMethod variants. Operators pick the mix per tenant.

items.definition: Phishing-resistant by construction. The signature is bound to your device + the origin. The server never sees a secret it could leak.

Server never stores a password. Lower phishing resistance than a passkey, higher recoverability when the device is lost. Tenants disable this method via forge-auth.toml if their threat model rules out shared-mailbox vectors.

Classic salted Argon2id with mandatory MFA (TOTP / WebAuthn / SMS / Email OTP / backup codes). Argon2id parameters tuned per the OWASP cheat-sheet; MFA factors enumerated as a closed enum so the call-site can't add a weak factor by accident.

sections.title: Bring your own provider
sections.body: forge-auth wraps a typed AuthProvider trait. Postgres-Argon2id, vault-WebAuthn, OAuth-2 / OIDC against any IdP — pick your impl, plug it in.

sections.title: Start free.
sections.lede: Solo plan is free forever. Team plan's first three seats are free.
